zh.lifeSummary: 當Giovanna Di San Severino於1340年出生在比利時海瑙特巴希利時，她的父親Guglielmo Sanseverino Signore di  Montesano51歲，母親Margherita de Scocca46歲。她和Louis d'Enghien Comte de Brienne至少有1個兒子和4個女兒。她去世於1393年在她的家鄉享年53歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Giovanna Di San Severino于1340年出生在比利时海瑙特巴希利时，她的父亲Guglielmo Sanseverino Signore di  Montesano51岁，母亲Margherita de Scocca46岁。她和Louis d'Enghien Comte de Brienne至少有1个儿子和4个女儿。她去世于1393年在她的家乡享年53岁。

nameMeanings.name: San
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Southeast Asian Khin, Pui, Sokhom, Heng, Kin, Kin Hong, Kun, Lai, Leong, Man, Ming, Ouk, Senh, Ly, Mui, Pha, Phal, Pham, Tha, Tran, Coong, Dam, Duy, Hanh, Noy, Hoeun, Noeun, Phath, Roeuth, Samoeun, Soeum, Sophat, Sovann, Yoeun, Pong, Savuth, Thai.
Burmese: from a personal name usually forming part of a compound name, from san ‘moon’, also ‘good luck’, or from a homonym meaning ‘spring (of water)’. See also 2 below.

Burmese: from a personal name usually forming part of a compound name, from hsan ‘to be extraordinary’. — Note: Since Burmese do not have hereditary surnames, these names (see also 1 above) were registered as such only after immigration of their bearers to the US.

variants.name: Shen
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Ming, Wen, You Qin, Ning, Jian, Ying, Li, Wei, Yi, Fei, Hui, Min, Chang, Chung, Tian, You, Sa, Yiping, Yu-Min.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 沈: (i) from Shen (沈), the name of an ancient state (possibly located in present-day Shanxi province) prior to the Xia dynasty (2070–1600  BC ). (ii) from the placename Shen (沈), the name of a fief (located in Pingyu in present-day Henan province) granted to Ji Zai, also known as Dan Shu, the tenth son of King Wen of Zhou (1152–1056  BC ). After the state was annexed by the state of Cai in 506  BC , people kept the name of the fiefdom as their surname. (iii) from Shen Lu (沈鹿), the name of a fief (located in Hubei province) granted to Zhen, son of King Zhuang of Chu (ruler of the state of Chu, died 591  BC ). His descendants adopted part of the name of the fiefdom, 沈, as their surname.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 申, meaning ‘state, explain’ in Chinese: (i) from Shen (申), an ancient state (located in Ningxia and Shaanxi provinces), also known as West Shen. After it was annexed by the state of Qin during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), some people used the name of the state as their surname. (ii) from Shen (申), the name of a state (located in Nanyang in Henan province), also known as Southern Shen, granted to Shen Bo (申伯, the Earl of Shen), uncle of King Xuan of Zhou (reigned 827–782  BC ). After the state was annexed by the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), people kept the name of the state as their surname. (iii) from the placename Shen (申), the name of a fief (located in Nanyang in Henan province) granted to Shen Shu, an official in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (iv) from the placename Shen (申), the name of a fief (located in Xiangcheng in Henan province) granted to Shen Hou (Marquis of Shen), an official in the state of Zheng (located in part of present-day Shaanxi and Henan province) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ).

nameMeanings.name: Severino
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Mario, Alfonso, Eusebia, Juan, Luz, Orlando, Rafael, Ramon, Adriana, Agueda, Agustina, Alba.  Italian Angelo, Antonio, Alfio, Amato, Bruna, Domenica, Elvio, Enrico, Filomena, Francesco, Gerlando, Manlio.
Italian and Spanish: from the personal name Severino (see Severin ).

variants.name: Severini
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Valentino, Aldo, Armando, Camillo, Egidio, Reno, Mario, Sal, Salvatore.
Italian: patronymic or plural form of   Severino  .

variants.name: Savarino
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Salvatore, Biagio, Carmela, Domenico, Gino, Giovani, Vincenzo.
Italian (Sicily): variant of   Severino  .

variants.name: Severin
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Christophe, Florien, Francois, Marcel, Monique, Olivier, Patrice.

German, Swedish, Italian (Veneto), Czech, Croatian, and Romanian; Ukrainian (standard transliteration Severyn); French (Séverin); Slovak (Severín): from a personal name, Latin Severinus, from the same root as Severus (see Severe ). This was borne by several early Christian saints, including bishops of Trier (2nd century), Cologne (4th century), and Bordeaux (5th century), and hermits of Paris (6th century) and Tivoli (8th century), as well as a 5th-century apostle of Austria.
Jewish (Ashkenazic): shortened form of Severinovsky, a habitational name for someone from Severinovka in Ukraine.

variants.name: Saverino
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Alfonso, Mario, Salvatore, Santo, Ciro, Francesco, Gaetano, Giulio, Salvator, Umberto, Vittorio.
Italian:
variant of   Severino  .

nameMeanings.name: Giovanna
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

variants.name: Jayne
meanings: Elaborated spelling of   Jane  , a form popularized no doubt by the American actress  Jayne Mansfield  (  Vera Jayne Palmer ,  1933–67 ) and British ice-dancer  Jayne Torvill  (  b. 1957 ).

variants.name: Jaine
meanings: Variant spelling of   Jane .

variants.name: Jain
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

nameMeanings.name: Di
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Diana   and   Diane .

variants.name: Diana
meanings: Name borne in Roman mythology by the goddess of the moon and of hunting, equivalent to the Greek Artemis. In mythology she is characterized as both beautiful and chaste. Her name is of ancient and uncertain derivation. It probably contains a first element that is also found in the name of the Greek god Dionysos ( see Dennis ) and the Latin name of the supreme god Jupiter. It was adopted in Britain during the Tudor period as a learned name, a borrowing from Latin influenced by the French form Diane . Although it was much used by Elizabethan poets celebrating the virgin goddess and alluding to the Virgin Queen, it was not particularly popular as a given name until the end of the 19th century. In earlier centuries some clergymen were reluctant to baptize girls with this pagan name, mindful of the riots against St Paul stirred up by worshippers of Diana of the Ephesians (Acts 19:24–41). In the late 20th century, its popularity received a boost because of its association with the late Diana , Princess of Wales ( 1961–97 ), who was renowned for her beauty, glamour, and compassion.

variants.name: Diane
meanings: form of   Diana  , now also widely used in the English-speaking world. It was especially popular among the Renaissance aristocracy, who loved hunting and were therefore proud to name their daughters after the classical goddess of the chase.

en.lifeSummary: When Giovanna Di San Severino was born in 1340, in Bassilly, Hainaut, Belgium, her father, Guglielmo Sanseverino Signore di  Montesano, was 51 and her mother, Margherita de Scocca, was 46. She had at least 1 son and 4 daughters with Louis d'Enghien Comte de Brienne. She died in 1393, in her hometown, at the age of 53.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Giovanna Di San Severino est née en 1340, à Bassilly, Hainaut, Belgique, son père, Guglielmo Sanseverino Signore di  Montesano, avait 51 ans et sa mère, Margherita de Scocca, avait 46 ans. Elle a eu au moins 1 fils et 4 filles avec Louis d'Enghien Comte de Brienne. Elle est décédée en 1393, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 53 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Giovanna Di San Severino 1340 in Bassilly, Hennegau, Belgien geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Guglielmo Sanseverino Signore di  Montesano 51 und ihre Mutter Margherita de Scocca 46. Sie hatte mit Louis d'Enghien Comte de Brienne mindestens 1 Sohn und 4 Töchter. Sie starb 1393 im Alter von 53 Jahren in ihrem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Giovanna Di San Severino è nata il nel 1340 nel Bassilly, Hainaut, Belgio, sua padre, Guglielmo Sanseverino Signore di  Montesano, aveva 51 anni e sua madre, Margherita de Scocca, aveva 46 anni. Lei avuto almeno 1 figlio e 4 figlie con Louis d'Enghien Comte de Brienne. Morì nel 1393, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 53 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Giovanna Di San Severinoが1340年にベルギーヘイナトバシリーで生まれた時，父Guglielmo Sanseverino Signore di  Montesanoは51歳，母Margherita de Scoccaは46歳であった。彼女はLouis d'Enghien Comte de Brienneとの間に少なくとも1人の息子と4人の娘をもうけた。彼女は1393年に故郷で53歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Giovanna Di San Severino(이)가 1340년 벨기에 하인오트 배시리에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Guglielmo Sanseverino Signore di  Montesano(은)는 51세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Margherita de Scocca(은)는 46세였다. 그녀는 Louis d'Enghien Comte de Brienne(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1393년 그녀의 고향에서 53세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Giovanna Di San Severino nasceu em 1340, em Bassilly, Hainaut, Bélgica, seu pai, Guglielmo Sanseverino Signore di  Montesano, tinha 51 anos e sua mãe, Margherita de Scocca, tinha 46 anos. Ela teve pelo menos 1 filho e 4 filhas com Louis d'Enghien Comte de Brienne. Ela faleceu em 1393, em sua cidade natal, com 53 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Giovanna Di San Severino родилась в 1340 г. в местности бассилли, ханаут, Бельгия, её отцу по имени Guglielmo Sanseverino Signore di  Montesano было 51 год, а матери, которую звали Margherita de Scocca, 46. У неё был по крайней мере 1 сын и 4 дочери от Louis d'Enghien Comte de Brienne. Она умерла в 1393 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 53 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Giovanna Di San Severino nació en 1340, en Bassilly, Hainaut, Bélgica, su papá, Guglielmo Sanseverino Signore di  Montesano, tenía 51 años y su mamá, Margherita de Scocca, tenía 46 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo y 4 hijas con Louis d'Enghien Comte de Brienne. Murió en 1393, en su pueblo, a la edad de 53 años.
